title: Outbound redirect endpoint with click tracking
description: Add GET /out/<listing_id> that looks up the listing's stored URL, resolves the (possibly affiliate) destination, records a click event, and issues a 302. Repoint _ui.html, _match_table.html, and auctions.html at this endpoint instead of linking listing rows directly.
acceptance_criteria: GET /out/<listing_id> 302-redirects to the listing's resolved destination URL
Unknown/missing listing_id returns 404, not a redirect to an empty/garbage URL
_ui.html (spotlight, deal-card), _match_table.html, and auctions.html link to url_for('listing_out', listing_id=...) instead of row['url'] directly
target="_blank" rel="noopener" is preserved on all changed links
When no affiliate resolver applies to a source, the redirect target equals the original listings.url unchanged
listings.url column is never written to by this feature — read-only lookup
Existing tests covering listing card rendering are updated, not broken, by the href change
notes: See docs/adr/0002-affiliate-link-redirect-and-tracking.md. Manual-assisted search links (manual.html, _project_detail_live.html manual-search list) are out of scope — they have no listing_id. Shipped 2026-07-08: url_for('listing_out', listing_id=row['id']) in the acceptance criteria above was a spec bug — _MATCH_SELECT never selects a plain 'id' column, so row['listing_id'] is used throughout instead. Also repointed offers.html and the duplicate-review dup_side macro (_project_detail_live.html), beyond the three templates named here, per the broader 'no template ever emits a marketplace URL' brief this shipped under. See docs/implementation-notes/2026-07-08-2135-marketplace-outbound-gateway-epic-101.md.
